section.heading: Damaged personal protective equipment: award to members separating from the Armed Forces and veterans

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.content: The Secretary of a military department, acting through a disposition service distribution center of the Defense Logistics Agency, may award to a covered individual the demilitarized PPE of that covered individual. The award of PPE under this section shall be without cost to the covered individual.

subparagraph.chapeau: a member of the armed forces—

clause.content: under the jurisdiction of the Secretary concerned; and
who is separating from the armed forces; or

subparagraph.content: a veteran who was under the jurisdiction of the Secretary concerned while a member of the armed forces.

paragraph.chapeau: The term “PPE” means personal protective equipment that was damaged in combat or otherwise—

subparagraph.content: during the deployment of a covered individual; and

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 115–232, div. A, title VI, § 623(a), Aug. 13, 2018, 132 Stat. 1800.)
